expectations.text: Document contains all 8 required section headings: Technique Overview, Attack Steps, Detection Opportunities, Data Sources & Log Fields, Pseudocode Queries, and MITRE ATT&CK Mapping
expectations.evidence: The document uses a stage-based narrative structure ('Attack Chain' with Stages 1-6) rather than the required section schema. 'Technique Overview' is absent (replaced by 'Overview' at line 3). 'Attack Steps' is absent (replaced by multi-stage 'Attack Chain'). 'Data Sources & Log Fields' is absent — log sources appear only as a brief subsection under 'Detection Opportunities' (line 183). 'Pseudocode Queries' is entirely absent. 'MITRE ATT&CK Mapping' is not a standalone section — the mapping is embedded in the 'Overview' block (lines 7-10). Only 'Detection Opportunities' matches a required heading.
T1053.003 is explicitly referenced at line 9 ('Technique: Scheduled Task/Job: Cron (T1053.003)') and in the References section at line 234 as a MITRE ATT&CK link.

expectations.text: At least one detection method is labeled with High, Medium, or Low fidelity
expectations.evidence: No detection method in the document is labeled with 'High', 'Medium', or 'Low' fidelity. The Detection Opportunities section (lines 156-197) uses plain categories ('File System', 'Process Behavior', 'Command-Line Indicators', 'Log Sources') without any fidelity classification.

expectations.text: Data sources section mentions auditd or journald or syslog as Linux-specific log sources
expectations.evidence: The Detection Opportunities 'Log Sources' subsection (line 183) lists '/var/log/syslog or /var/log/cron', '/var/log/auth.log', and 'Auditd rules on cron file paths'. Example auditd rules are also provided (lines 192-197).

expectations.text: Pseudocode queries use UDM-style field names like event.type, process.name, file.path rather than vendor-specific syntax
expectations.evidence: The document has no 'Pseudocode Queries' section. Detection examples (lines 176-197) are written as plain command-line patterns and raw auditd rule syntax (e.g., '-w /etc/cron.d/ -p wa -k cron_persistence'), not UDM-style pseudocode with dotted field names.

suggestions: Restructure using the required section headings: Technique Overview, Attack Steps, Detection Opportunities, Data Sources & Log Fields, Pseudocode Queries, MITRE ATT&CK Mapping.
Add a standalone Pseudocode Queries section with UDM-style dotted field name syntax.
Add fidelity labels (High/Medium/Low) to each detection method.
Move the MITRE ATT&CK mapping into its own top-level section rather than embedding it in the overview.
Add a dedicated Data Sources & Log Fields section separate from Detection Opportunities.
eval_feedback.overall: Below threshold. The document covers the attack technique well with good operational detail and code examples, but fails structural compliance on 3 of 5 assertions. It lacks the required section schema, fidelity classification, and UDM-style pseudocode queries.
